title: OnePlus Pad 4 India launch confirmed: Check display, processor, battery, launch date and features
snippet: OnePlus is set to launch the OnePlus Pad 4 in India on April 30, 2026. It features a 13.2-inch 3.4K display, Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor, up to 12GB RAM, and 13,380 mAh battery with 80W fast charging

title: Inditex data breach: Zara owner Inditex reports major data breach exposing customer transaction records
snippet: Zara owner Inditex reported a data breach involving a third party. The company said latest breach exposed transaction-related information but no personal details.

title: Meet Happy Oyster: Alibaba launches new AI video model that turns text prompts into playable 3D worlds
snippet: Alibabahas launched the Happy Oyster AI model. The new model is capable of generating interactive 3D environments and videos in real time, allowing users to direct or explore evolving scenes with consistent physics

title: ‘Your job is changing on you’: LinkedIn says AI isn't killing hiring, but will rewrite your skillset by 2030
snippet: A LinkedIn executive has revealed that AI isn't behind the hiring slowdown and instead blames it on rising interest rates.

title: Nothing launches the ultimate Android-to-Mac transfer app, quietly takes it down hours later
snippet: Nothing has taken down the Warp app from Android and Chrome soon after the launch. The company is yet to share a reason for the removal of the app.

title: YouTube finally lets you turn off Shorts: Check out exact settings to clean up your feed
snippet: YouTube now allows users to completely remove Shorts videos from the app, a feature added in 2020. This option is part of the Shorts Feed Limit feature which was first introduced last year.

title: Google brings the Gemini app to Mac, Sundar Pichai says it was built by AI
snippet: Google has released the Gemini app for Mac, allowing users to access its AI assistant via a keyboard shortcut and share screens for contextual help. The app was built by Google's own AI coding tool Antigravity, Sundar Pichai revealed

title: Quick commerce turning to reset lane as profit push tempers growth metrics
snippet: After several quarters of breakneck expansion, quick commerce majors Blinkit and Instamart are expected to report moderation in growth in the January-March quarter, as they sharpen focus on margin improvement and profitability, analysts and industry executives said.
